core_mechanism: continuous scale-mixture of Gaussian kernels — integrating a family of Gaussian propagators against a mixing measure over their variance parameter, with a constraint on that measure that preserves the convolution (Chapman-Kolmogorov) semigroup structure
brief_reason: The abstract builds distributions as superpositions of Gaussians of different variances v=σ² smeared by a mixing distribution over v, and derives the smearing measures that do not destroy the Markov semigroup property, which is precisely a variance-mixture-of-Gaussians construction constrained to keep a convolution semigroup.
